message: beads: file perf benchmark audit blocker

files.patch:
@@ -700,6 +700,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-vqmz","title":"Reject symlinked DB recovery before WAL quarantine","description":"Round-2 review found that symlinked beads.db recovery now refuses to rebuild the database path, but open_sqlite_storage_with_recovery_strategy still calls quarantine_truncated_wal_sidecar before the symlink rejection inside backup/cleanup. A symlinked beads.db with a truncated beads.db-wal can therefore create .br_recovery artifacts and move sidecars even though recovery should be a no-op refusal. Move the symlink guard ahead of pre-open sidecar quarantine and add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:21:00.931167361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:27:30.043330254Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:27:30.042997851Z","close_reason":"Skipped truncated WAL quarantine for symlinked database paths and extended symlink recovery coverage so refusal leaves sidecars and targets untouched.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-vs6b","title":"CLI ready.rs tests logging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T20:56:00.809817358Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T20:56:09.925438977Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T20:56:09.925438977Z","close_reason":"Added per-test logging/init_test_logging to ready.rs tests; ran cargo fmt/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-vs6b","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-vlt","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-vsx1","title":"Define ESTIMATED_BYTES_PER_ISSUE const in sync/mod.rs","description":"SUBTASK 3.2: The value 500 is used twice in sync/mod.rs for Vec pre-allocation heuristic: (file_size / 500) as usize at lines 2695 and 2988. This estimates how many issues a JSONL file contains based on its byte size, to pre-allocate the Vec and avoid repeated reallocations.

CHANGES:
- Add: const ESTIMATED_BYTES_PER_ISSUE: u64 = 500;
- Replace both (file_size / 500) with (file_size / ESTIMATED_BYTES_PER_ISSUE)
- Add comment: // Heuristic for Vec pre-allocation; a typical issue serializes to ~300-700 bytes

WHY 500: A minimal issue JSON record is ~200 bytes (just required fields). A typical one with description, labels, and comments is ~500-700 bytes. 500 is a conservative middle ground that slightly over-allocates (better than under-allocating and triggering reallocations).

SCOPE: Only used within sync/mod.rs, so a module-private const is fine.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:44.379362702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:27.818159012Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:27.817505999Z","close_reason":"ESTIMATED_BYTES_PER_ISSUE const added in sync/mod.rs, both pre-allocation sites updated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"beads_rust-vuk2","title":"[MEDIUM] Restore storage_perf benchmark auditability under rch","description":"Round 3 perf audit could not establish benchmark baseline/regression signal.

Repro:
1. Run: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR="/data/tmp/cargo-target" cargo bench --bench storage_perf -- --quick
   Result: cargo reports no bench target named storage_perf; Cargo.toml exposes benches/storage_perf.rs as bench target "benchmarks".
2. Run equivalent target: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR="/data/tmp/cargo-target" cargo bench --bench benchmarks -- --quick
   Result: rch remote vmi1152480 lacks nightly-2026-02-19 and falls back local; local build uses .cargo/config.toml path patches into ../frankensqlite, where current sibling checkout fails in fsqlite-btree/src/cursor.rs with missing StackEntry field mutation_counter at lines 3356, 5774, 5813, 6022, and 6065.

Gap audit:
- JSONL import/export throughput is benchmarked only for 100/500/1000/2000/5000 issues, not the 10K target listed in benches/storage_perf.rs.
- ready-query coverage stops at 1000 issues / 2000 deps, not the 10K/20K deep graph target listed in the header.
- MCP serve tool latency has no Criterion bench group under benches/storage_perf.rs or src/mcp/.

Baseline commits considered: def55da perf(sync) stream JSONL export, 3f206ff sync/mcp lock-error split, 7a1fc87 MCP dirty-storage flush. Need first make the benchmark invocation reliable under rch/current dependency state, then add/compare 10K JSONL, 10K/20K ready graph, and MCP tool latency measurements before filing any regression-specific bead.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:49:01.198885127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:49:01.198885127Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-vwuc","title":"Fix changelog --json output mode","description":"Fresh-eye audit found src/cli/commands/changelog.rs branches incorrectly: when json mode is requested without --robot, it renders rich/plain text instead of JSON. This bead tracks fixing the command output path and adding regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:25:08.643975652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T16:28:43.894424838Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T16:28:43.893688008Z","close_reason":"Already fixed: changelog.rs has resolve_render_mode(json, ctx.mode()) which forces ChangelogRenderMode::Json when json=true (including --json without --robot). The JSON branch at line 169-179 has a fallback to println! when ctx.is_json() is false, ensuring JSON output regardless of TTY state.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-vx68","title":"Fix sync --robot status output and auto-import prefix validation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:05:30.616355116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T16:05:50.229014656Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T16:05:50.228989429Z","close_reason":"Fixed sync robot status output + auto-import prefix validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-w1i","title":"lint Command Implementation","description":"# lint Command (template validation)

## Purpose
Read-only validation of required template headings in issue descriptions. This is classic bd behavior (substring match, case-insensitive), not a strict Markdown parser.

## CLI
```
br lint [issue-id...] [--type <type>] [--status <status>]
```
Defaults:
- If no IDs: lint open issues only.
- Daemon mode excluded; direct storage only.

## Required Sections (classic)
- **bug**: "Steps to Reproduce" + "Acceptance Criteria"
- **task/feature**: "Acceptance Criteria"
- **epic**: "Success Criteria"
- Other types: no requirements

Matching rules:
- Case-insensitive substring search.
- Markdown prefixes (`#`, `##`) stripped before matching.

## JSON Output
```json
{
  "total": 3,
  "issues": 2,
  "results": [
    { "id": "bd-abc", "title": "Fix", "type": "bug", "missing": ["## Steps to Reproduce"], "warnings": 1 }
  ]
}
```
Notes:
- `results` includes only issues with warnings.
- JSON mode exits 0 even with warnings.
- Human mode exits 1 if warnings exist.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Required headings matched case-insensitively.
- JSON and human outputs match bd.

## Tests
- Issue descriptions with and without required headings.
- Exit code differences between JSON and human modes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:50.930149167Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:36:25.244001818Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:36:25.244001818Z","close_reason":"Lint command fully implemented: template section validation for bug/task/feature/epic types with case-insensitive matching, JSON output support, exit codes per spec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
